title: Elixir NIF wrapper module (socket_handoff.ex)
description: Create lib/blue_green/socket_handoff.ex with use Rustler, otp_app: :blue_green, crate: :socket_handoff. Wrap send_fd/2 and recv_fd/1 with typespecs and error handling.

title: Scaffold Rustler project in native/socket_handoff/
description: Run mix rustler.new or manually create native/socket_handoff/ with Cargo.toml (deps: rustler, nix with socket+uio+net features) and src/lib.rs stub. Add {:rustler, ~0.37.3} to mix.exs deps. Verify mix compile loads NIF.

title: Rust NIF: SCM_RIGHTS FD passing over UDS
description: Rustler NIF project in native/socket_handoff/ implementing send_fd and recv_fd as dirty IO NIFs using the nix crate's sendmsg/recvmsg with SCM_RIGHTS ancillary data. This is the kernel-level foundation for the entire hot-handoff demo.

title: Handler GenServer: version-tagged echo over socket
description: Create lib/blue_green/handler.ex GenServer. init receives {fd, version, uds_path}. Wraps FD via FdUtil.wrap_fd/1. Runs recv loop in handle_continue: reads line from client, responds VERSION N: <line>. On {:handoff, target_uds_path} cast: extracts FD, calls SocketHandoff.send_fd, stops self.

title: Demo integration and test client
description: Test client script, end-to-end wiring, README update. Proves zero-downtime TCP handoff visually.

title: Application logic: handler + orchestrator
description: GenServer handler (version-tagged echo server) and orchestrator (TCP listener, :peer node lifecycle, upgrade trigger). This is the blue/green control plane.

title: NIF unit test: send/recv FD round-trip on same node
description: Elixir test: open a TCP socket, extract FD, spawn Task for recv_fd, call send_fd, verify received FD is valid by writing/reading through socket.open/2.

title: Test client script (test_client.exs)
description: Create test_client.exs Elixir script. Connects TCP localhost:4000, sends 'ping N' every 2 seconds in a loop, prints each response. Shows VERSION 1 -> VERSION 2 transition. Exits after 20 iterations or on disconnect.

title: Implement recv_fd NIF (UDS bind+listen + SCM_RIGHTS recvmsg)
description: Dirty IO NIF: takes (path: String). Unlinks stale socket, binds UDS at path, listens(1), accepts one connection, calls recvmsg to extract SCM_RIGHTS FD from cmsg, unlinks socket, returns {:ok, fd}. Must handle cleanup on error.

title: FD extraction and wrapping helpers (fd_util.ex)
description: Create lib/blue_green/fd_util.ex. extract_fd/1 uses :prim_inet.getfd/1 on gen_tcp socket (requires inet_backend: :inet). wrap_fd/1 calls :socket.open(fd, %{domain: :inet, type: :stream, protocol: :tcp, dup: false}) to reconstruct Erlang socket from raw FD.

title: Implement send_fd NIF (UDS connect + SCM_RIGHTS sendmsg)
description: Dirty IO NIF: takes (path: String, fd: i32). Connects to UDS at path, builds cmsg with SCM_RIGHTS containing fd, calls sendmsg with 1-byte data payload, closes UDS connection. Returns :ok or {:error, reason}.

title: End-to-end integration test and README
description: Wire full flow: iex -S mix, start orchestrator, run test client, call Orchestrator.upgrade(), verify version switch. Update README.md with architecture diagram, setup steps, and demo walkthrough.

title: Orchestrator GenServer: TCP listener + peer lifecycle
description: Create lib/blue_green/orchestrator.ex GenServer. start_link opens TCP :4000 (inet_backend: :inet). Spawns acceptor task. On accept: extracts FD, sends to active child UDS. Manages :peer nodes (v1 on start, v2 on upgrade). upgrade/0 API: starts v2 peer, tells v1 handler to handoff, stops v1 peer. Peer nodes get code paths from Code.get_path().

title: Elixir FD plumbing layer
description: Elixir modules wrapping the NIF and providing extract_fd/wrap_fd helpers. Bridges gen_tcp ports to raw FDs and back to :socket.t() references.
